title: Scottish Government Urged to Act on Prestwick Airport Concerns

sections.heading: Greens call for action
sections.content: Scottish Green MSP Patrick Harvie said ministers must act after BBC reporting linked some US military aircraft stopping at Prestwick Airport to the US-Israeli airstrikes against Iran. Harvie said the people of Scotland deserved clarity and reassurance that the country would not be complicit in escalating the conflict and argued that the Scottish government now had to set out what it knew about the flights.

sections.heading: Questions over Prestwick policy
sections.content: Harvie said that in 2024 the Scottish government had made clear that Israeli military aircraft would not be serviced at Prestwick Airport. He argued that ministers therefore had to explain why the same scrutiny and standards were not being applied to US Air Force aircraft if they were potentially supporting the same military operations. He also called for transparency over what discussions had taken place with the UK government and US authorities.

sections.heading: Pressure on ministers
sections.content: According to the Scottish Greens statement, the presence of US military aircraft potentially refuelling Israeli aircraft would make Prestwick a staging post for activity linked to the conflict. Harvie said John Swinney had already indicated he would consider banning US use of the airport if the evidence justified it and argued that he now had enough information to act. He said ministers should make clear whether Scotland's infrastructure would continue to be used in connection with operations in the Middle East.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Prestwick: Scottish Government must act following US flight revelations
